title: Trace a late checksum diagnostic back to a bounded-write overwrite
prompt:
A capture worker has started reporting checksum failures only for feeds whose source tag fills its fixed field. The report is emitted at the end of capture, so the checksum comparison and its logger were initially blamed. One representative incident was initialized with source `north-sensor`, expected checksum `9f2c7a10`, and actual checksum `9f2c7a10`, yet the late diagnostic was:

```
checksum mismatch after capture: source="north-senso" expected= actual=9f2c7a10
```

The checksum is still intact immediately before the source tag is stored. A guard probe around a standalone source destination narrows the evidence further:

```
source length                 guard before       guard after
11 (`north-senso`)            a5 a5 a5 a5        a5 a5 a5 a5
12 (`north-sensor`)           a5 a5 a5 a5        00 a5 a5 a5
```

Treat the late checksum message as a symptom and repair the earlier corruption in `capture.c`. A source destination's capacity includes its terminating NUL: capacity 12 stores at most 11 source bytes, always NUL-terminates when capacity is nonzero, returns the number of source bytes stored, and performs no write when capacity is zero. Longer input is truncated by the same rule. In a capture record, checksum state immediately follows the logical source destination, so crossing that boundary corrupts the later comparison.

Keep the downstream behavior precise. Equal eight-character checksums must produce no diagnostic; a real mismatch must continue to return false and format exactly `checksum mismatch after capture: source="<source>" expected=<expected> actual=<actual>\n`. Do not silence, defer, or special-case the checksum check, and do not change record layout or public interfaces. `make test` must pass offline. `.gitignore`, `Makefile`, `capture.h`, and `test_capture.c` are protected and must not be modified.

full_distill.primary_skill: treat a delayed checksum log as a corruption symptom, use boundary-canary evidence to identify the earlier one-byte overwrite, and repair the bounded write without weakening downstream diagnostics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero: exact-capacity and overlong source copies report 12 stored bytes, omit the in-bounds terminator, and change the first post-destination guard byte from 0xa5 to 0x00; the same write clears the first byte of a capture record's expected checksum and creates a false late mismatch
full_distill.success_contract: all nonzero source destinations reserve space for an in-bounds NUL and preserve every byte beyond their declared capacity, record initialization leaves adjacent checksum state intact, equal checksums stay quiet, and genuine mismatches retain the exact expected/source/actual diagnostic
adversarial_factors: the observed failure is a late checksum log, but an equal expected/actual pair and a phase-local guard transition place the cause in earlier source storage
the protected suite probes exact-capacity and longer input with multiple canary bytes, so changing the checksum comparison or logger cannot repair the boundary violation

the source and checksum occupy offsets in one backing array, making the logical overwrite deterministic without relying on allocator layout, endianness, sanitizers, or undefined out-of-allocation access
a legitimate mismatch is asserted byte-for-byte while a match must clear stale output, rejecting fixes that suppress or coarsen downstream diagnostics
zero-capacity and one-byte-short boundary cases preserve existing behavior and reject broad rewrites that introduce new writes or truncation errors
